name: cellxgene-census
description: Query CZ CELLxGENE Census (61M+ cells). Filter by cell type/tissue/disease, retrieve expression data, integrate with scanpy/PyTorch, for population-scale single-cell analysis.
capabilities: Query single-cell expression data by cell type, tissue, or disease
Access standardized metadata for 61+ million cells from human and mouse
Integrate Census data with scanpy for standard bioinformatics workflows
Use PyTorch data loaders for machine learning model training
Perform out-of-core processing for large-scale queries exceeding RAM
Retrieve gene expression matrices with flexible filtering options
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with Python code examples. Static scanner flagged documentation patterns (code block syntax, text strings) as security issues due to misinterpretation. All findings are false positives. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or environment variable access exists.
valueStatement: Researchers need efficient access to population-scale single-cell data for genomics studies. This skill provides AI-powered access to the CZ CELLxGENE Census with optimized query patterns and integration guides for downstream analysis.
limitations: Requires internet connection to access Census data repository
Large queries may require significant RAM or out-of-core processing
Only includes data from CZ CELLxGENE Discover following standardization criteria
Version control requires explicit version specification for reproducibility

useCases.title: Explore cell types in tissues
useCases.description: Query cell type distributions across tissues using metadata filters and aggregation functions

useCases.title: Build cell type classifiers
useCases.description: Train machine learning models on Census data using PyTorch integration for biomarker discovery

useCases.title: Cross-dataset analysis
useCases.description: Integrate multiple datasets for population-scale studies with scanpy workflows

promptTemplates.title: Explore available data
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the unique cell types in the brain tissue from the Census. Use the cellxgene-census skill to query metadata with is_primary_data == True filter.

promptTemplates.title: Query gene expression
promptTemplates.prompt: Query expression data for CD4, CD8A, and CD19 genes in T cells and B cells from lung tissue. Use cellxgene-census to retrieve AnnData objects.

promptTemplates.title: Train ML model
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PyTorch dataloader using cellxgene-census experimental ml module to train a cell type classifier on liver cell data with 80-20 train-test split.

promptTemplates.title: Large-scale analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use axis_query with out-of-core processing to iterate through brain cell expression data in chunks for memory-efficient analysis.

bestPractices: Always include is_primary_data == True filter to avoid counting duplicate cells across datasets
Specify census_version explicitly for reproducible analyses in production workflows
Use context manager (with statement) for automatic resource cleanup when opening Census
antiPatterns: Loading entire Census without filters causes memory overflow
Ignoring dataset presence matrix leads to missing gene data
Using free text filters instead of ontology terms reduces query consistency

faq.question: Which organisms are supported?
faq.answer: The Census includes data for Homo sapiens (human) and Mus musculus (mouse) organisms only.

faq.question: How many cells can I query at once?
faq.answer: Small queries under 100k cells work in memory. Larger queries require axis_query with out-of-core processing.

faq.question: Can I use this with scanpy?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides integration patterns for loading Census data directly into AnnData objects for scanpy workflows.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: The skill only reads public Census data. No user data is transmitted. The library connects to the CZ CELLxGENE data repository.

faq.question: Why are some genes missing?
faq.answer: Genes may be filtered during Census construction or not measured in all datasets. Use the presence matrix to check coverage.

faq.question: How does this compare to downloading datasets directly?
faq.answer: Census provides standardized, versioned access with unified metadata. Queries filter data server-side reducing download needs.
